steps.title: Connect effort to thoughtfulness
steps.content: The passage starts with a gift-giving norm: the time spent obtaining a gift is treated as a sign of thoughtfulness. It then contrasts digital gift cards, which can be bought online from anywhere, with physical gift cards, which sometimes require an in-person purchase. The finding must explain why givers still see physical cards as more socially acceptable.

steps.title: Reject unsupported assumptions
steps.content: Choice A shifts the issue to effort required to use digital gift cards, but the norm concerns effort required to obtain a gift. Choice B contradicts the passage because recipients view digital cards as superior in terms of usage. Choice C says physical cards are less desirable because they require unnecessary effort, but the passage describes physical cards as more socially acceptable to give.

steps.title: Use the stated norm
steps.content: Choice D follows the passage's logic: because digital cards are easy to obtain, gift givers may see them as lower-effort and therefore assume recipients will appreciate them less than physical cards. That explains the social preference for physical cards even though recipients like the usability of digital cards. The answer is D.
